creator: mikea@mikea.ath.cx
text:
during boot, while kernel is writing device discovery
	lines to console, system falls into kernel debugger
	with the following on the screen:
ppi0: <Parallel I/O> on ppbus0
Fatal trap 12: page fault while in kernel mode
mp_lock=00000003; cpuid=0; lapic.id=00000000
fault_virtualaddress	= 0x4006
faultcode		= supervisor read; page not present
instruction pointer	= 0x8:0xc0324906
stack pointer		= 0c10:0xc04d42ae0
frame pointer		= 0c10:0xc04d2b0c
code segment		= base 0x0, limit 0xfffff, type 0x1b
			= DPL 0, pres 1, def32 1; gran 1
processor eflags	= interrupt enabled, resume, IOPL=0
current process		= 0 (swapper)
interrupt mask		= net tty bio cam <- SMP:XXX
kernel			= type 12 trap, code=0
stopped at bcmp+0x16: 	repecmpsl 		(%esi),%es:(%edi)

Kernel compiled before yesterday's cvsup works fine. Recompiled
and reinstalled new kernel to see if the fault was repeatable.
It is: the new kernel fails with the same symptoms.

This line had just been written to console from a boot -v:
"isa_compat: didn't get memory for le"

Fix: 

I got it to go away by removing from the kernel config file every
device not physically present, and by setting the following
options:
options               MAXDSIZ="(512*1024*1024)"
options               DFLDSIZ="(512*1024*1024)"
which is the amount of RAM on my MP system. 

I have preserved the failing kernel and the config file that was
used to build it.
How-To-Repeat: 	
	Compile and boot a kernel with source from cvsup at 22Jun2001 14:22
	using my kernel config file MPFW7
